question: In how many ways can the word MACICITA be arranged?
solution:
MACICITA is an eight letter word = 8!

Since we have repeating letters, we have to divide to remove duplicates accordingly. There are 2A, 2C, 2I

∴ 8!/2!2!2!

question: If a rod 10cm in length was measured as 10.5cm, calculate the percentage error
solution:
Actual measurement = 10cm

approximated value of measurement = 10.5cm

% error = (Actual measurement − Approximated/Actual measure) × 100

= (10−10.5)/10 × 100

= −0.5/10 × 100

ignore -sign i.e take absolute value

= 0.5/10 × 100

= 5 %

question: In the figure, find x
solution:
Sum of angle at a point = 360°

2x + 3x + 4x = 360

9x = 360

x = 360/9


x = 40°

question: Find ∫(x<sup>2</sup>  + 3x − 5)dx
solution:
∫x<sup>n</sup>dx = x<sub>n+1</sub>/n+1


∫dx = x + k

where k is constant

∫(x<sup>2</sup>  + 3x − 5)dx

∫x<sup>2</sup>  dx + ∫3xdx − ∫5dx

(2<sub>2</sub> + 1)/(2 + 1)
 + 3x<sup>1+1</sup>/(1+1)
 − 5x + k

x<sub>3</sub>/3 + 3x<sub>2</sub>/2 − 5x + k

question: y is inversely proportional to x and y is 6 when x = 7. Find the constant of the variation
solution:
Y ∝ 1/2


Y = 6, X = 7

Y = k/x
 where k is constant

6 = k/7


k = 42

question: Find the gradient of the line joining the points (3, 2) and (1, 4)
solution:
Gradient of line joining points (3, 2), (1, 4)

Gradient = Change in Y/Change in X


= y<sub>2</sub> −Y<sub>1</sub>/ x<sub>2</sub> −x<sub>1</sub> 


(X<sub>1</sub> , Y<sub>1</sub> ) = (3, 2)

(X<sub>2</sub> , Y<sub>2</sub> ) = (1, 4)

Gradient = (4−2)/(1+3)


= 2/−2


= −1

question: A car dealer bought a second-hand car for 250,000 and spent N 70,000 refurbishing it. He then sold the car for N400,000. What is the percentage gain?
solution:
Total Cost Price = N(250,000 + 70,000)

= N 320,000

Selling Price = N 400,000(Given)

Gain = Selling Price - Cost Price

= 400,000 - 320,000

= 80,000

% gain = Gain/Cost Price
 × 100

= (80,000/320,000) × 100

Gain % = 25%

question: Find the sum of the range and the mode of the set of numbers 10, 9, 10, 9, 8, 7, 7, 10, 8, 10, 8, 4, 6, 9, 10, 9, 7, 10, 6, 5
solution:
Range = Highest Number - Lowest Number

Mode is the number with highest occurrence
10, 9, 10, 9, 8, 7, 7, 10, 8, 4, 6,, 9, 10, 9, 7, 10, 6, 5

Range = 10 − 4 = 6

Mode = 10

Sum of range and mode = range + mode = 6 + 10

= 16

question: The pie chart shows the allocation of money to each sector in a farm. The total amount allocated to the farm is ₦ 80 000. Find the amount allocated to fertilizer
solution:
Total angle at a point = 3600

∴ To get the angle occupied by fertilizer we have,

40 + 50 + 80 + 70 + 30 + fertilizer(x) = 360

270 + x = 360

x = 360 - 270

x = 90

Total amount allocated to the farm
= ₦ 80,000

∴Amount allocated to the fertilizer

= (fertilizer (angle) × Total amount)/total angle


= 90/360 × 80,000

= ₦20,000

question: The time taken to a piece of work is inversely proportional to the number of men employed. If it takes 45 men to do a piece of work in 5 days, how long will it take 25 men?
solution: Let t be the time taken Let m the number of men  t ∝1/m t = k/m (k = proportionality constant) when m =45, t=5.    5=k/45.   k =225 When we have 25 men, the number of days will be  t = k/m =225/25  t = 9days

question: Find the range of the following set of numbers 0.4, −0.4, 0.3, 0.47, −0.53, 0.2 and −0.2
solution:
0.4, −0.4, 0.3, 0.47, −0.53, 0.2, −0.2

Range is the difference between the highest and lowest value

i.e Highest − Lowest

− 0.53, −0.4, −0.2, 0.2, 0.3, 0.4, 0.47

0.47 is the highest

− 0.53 is the lowest

∴ = 0.47 − (− 0.53)

∴0.47 + 0.53

= 1.0

question: If y = x Sin x, find dy/dx when x = π/2
solution:
y = xsinx

dy/dx = 1 sin x + x cos x


= sin x + x cos x


At  x = π/2


= sin π/2 + π/2 cos π/2


= 1 + π/2 × 0

= 1
